measurement_model.description: Per-operation timing for the USDC binary-format unit. Times validate_header on a small fixture (baseline; expect sub-microsecond), decode_lz4_buffer on 64 KiB synthetic payloads in both 0-chunk and N-chunk forms, and decode_compressed_int_array on 8K-element int32/int64 arrays. Throughput floors are conservative first cuts; first benchmark runs will calibrate. Consumed by harness/handle_benchmark.py through the usdc-binary-format-unit scope's benchmark_adapter_cmd.

targets.notes: Header validation should be sub-microsecond on a warm cache; this baseline catches catastrophic regressions like per-call file I/O
64 KiB decompressed; LZ4 typically delivers >2 GB/s on a single core, so 200 us = ~330 MB/s is a wide safety margin around a byte-at-a-time interpreter
4 x 16 KiB chunks; per-chunk envelope bookkeeping adds some cost but should not break the throughput floor
8192 int32 elements; tests outer LZ4 decode + codepoint table dispatch + delta running sum
8192 int64 elements; codepoint width pairs are i16/i32/i64 instead of i8/i16/i32

ratios.rationale: multi-chunk envelope adds bookkeeping per chunk but should not double throughput cost on equal payload size
int64 path moves twice the bytes per element but should not exceed 2x the int32 cost; higher ratios indicate per-element branching or width-pair confusion
